query: Plan the implementation of a feature from .specs/features/feature-x/spec.md. Orient with Beads first, map every acceptance criterion to executable work, validate the graph, and identify the first claimable task.
expected_skill_contribution: Runs bd prime, translates spec AC IDs into acceptance-linked Beads tasks, validates the dependency graph, and hands off the first claimable task.
expected_behavior: Runs bd prime before reading planning state or creating tasks
Maps each spec AC ID to a task acceptance criterion with an assignee and priority
Creates and validates a dependency graph, then reports the first claimable task in the implementation handoff
baseline_gaps: Layer 2 agents+skills baseline may mention planning steps, but the plan recipe requires the ordered chain bd prime → AC extraction from spec.md → Beads task graph creation → bd graph/bd ready validation → implementation handoff.
Layer 2 agents+skills baseline does not require the recipe handoff fields: plan table, first claimable bead, and recommended /implement next step.

query: Plan a multi-component feature whose spec has several AC IDs, architecture work, implementation slices, review, and verification. Produce an ordered Beads graph without duplicate or unreachable work.
expected_skill_contribution: Decomposes multiple AC IDs into phased tasks with needs/partOf dependencies and verifies graph reachability.
expected_behavior: Runs bd prime and checks existing ready work before creating any task
Preserves acceptance traceability by linking every task to a spec AC ID
Uses dependency needs-language across architecture, implementation, review, and verification
Runs graph validation to reject cycles and unreachable nodes before handoff
baseline_gaps: Layer 2 agents+skills baseline may produce a task list, but the plan recipe requires dependencies to use needs: and partOf: semantics across architecture, implementation, review, and verification.
Layer 2 agents+skills baseline does not enforce the recipe validation gate that rejects cycles or unreachable nodes before handoff.

query: Break down a large cross-cutting epic from its formal spec. Include architecture constraints, more than ten graph nodes, implementation ownership boundaries, review and verification, and require approval before implementation begins.
expected_skill_contribution: Builds and validates a large spec-anchored task graph, adds the human approval gate, and emits a complete implementation handoff.
expected_behavior: Runs bd prime and checks the constitution before decomposing the epic
Maps all acceptance criteria and AC IDs to assigned tasks with explicit dependencies and non-overlapping implementation scopes
Performs an additional graph validation pass for more than ten nodes and fixes any cycle or unreachable node
Creates a human approval gate that blocks the first implementation task
Produces the required plan table and implementation handoff with epic ID, task count, and first claimable bead
baseline_gaps: Layer 2 agents+skills baseline may decompose a large epic, but the plan recipe escalates graphs with more than ten nodes to an additional reachability and cycle review before handoff.
Layer 2 agents+skills baseline does not require a human approval gate that blocks the first implementation task for Large/Complex scope.
